message:
perf(mvcc): eager version chain pruning and GC horizon SHM publication

Two related improvements to MVCC version chain lifecycle management:

## Eager post-commit version chain pruning

Previously, proactive version chain compaction during commit was
capped at 16 pages and only triggered when chain length exceeded an
adaptive threshold. This left stale version chains lingering when the
GC horizon had already advanced past all pinning snapshots.

The new `post_commit_version_maintenance` method detects when the
current GC horizon is >= the committing transaction's snapshot high
watermark. In that case, ALL written pages are eagerly pruned (not
just the first 16), because no active reader can possibly reference
the superseded versions. When the horizon has not advanced, the
previous capped-at-16 adaptive behavior is preserved.

This eliminates the common pattern where a single writer accumulates
long version chains between GC sweeps simply because the adaptive
threshold was not yet exceeded.

## GC horizon SHM publication

The `publish_cached_gc_horizon` method now also writes the horizon
value to the shared memory (SHM) segment via `shm.store_gc_horizon`.
This allows cross-process readers to observe the current GC horizon
without taking the active_snapshot_high_counts lock, which is
important for the pager's single-connection fast path to know whether
snapshot-pinned pages can be safely recycled.

## publish_write_set signature change

The method now accepts `&[PageNumber]` instead of computing pages from
txn.write_set internally. The caller clones the page list before the
commit path begins, so that post-commit maintenance can reference the
same page set after the transaction state has been cleared. The
commit_index update is also batched via `batch_update` instead of
per-page `update` calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
